title: C1 Raw-stream envelope v0 (learn-loop MVP, audio-first)
description: C1 has TWO legs. (1) BLOB LEG: recording writes the raw chunk bytes to storage /raw FIRST (PUT /raw/blobs, idempotent on chunk_id); storage mints an opaque blob_ref. (2) ENVELOPE LEG (this schema): recording then PUSHes this JSON envelope to data-processing. DELIVERY SEMANTICS: push, at-least-once; consumers (data-processing, storage /raw) are idempotent on chunk_id (the dedup key); ordering + gap detection use (stream_id, sequence), which is dense, zero-based and increments by exactly 1 within a stream (any break, including a non-zero first-seen value, = lost chunk(s)); the blob is durable in /raw BEFORE the envelope is first emitted, so blob_ref does not dangle at emit — but consumers (including the version-forward reprocess path) MUST tolerate a since-deleted blob, because /raw deletion (delete-last-N, right-to-be-forgotten) and re-pull-by-ref both exist. v0.0 exercises modality 'audio' (computer mic); the shape carries all four modalities.

device_id.description: Stable per capture device (v0: this computer's mic).

stream_id.description: One continuous capture session (mic-open->close). MUST be globally unique — client-minted like chunk_id (e.g. a ULID), never a reusable per-device boot/session counter — so (stream_id, sequence) cannot collide across concurrent devices. The namespace within which sequence is dense and gaps are detected.

sequence.description: The first chunk of a stream_id has sequence 0 and each subsequent chunk increments by exactly 1 (strictly increasing, dense, zero-based within stream_id). Ordering + gap detection: any break in this dense run — including a first-seen value greater than 0 — is lost chunk(s) ('zero silent loss'). NOT the dedup key.

chunk_id.description: Client-minted stable id for this chunk (e.g. ULID); constant across retries. THE DEDUP KEY: data-processing and storage /raw are idempotent on it.

codec.description: MIME/codec token of the blob, e.g. 'audio/ogg;codecs=opus', 'audio/wav'.

t_start.description: Chunk start, device wall-clock RFC3339 UTC. The time-spine axis.

t_end.description: Chunk end, device wall-clock RFC3339 UTC.

blob_ref.description: Opaque ref storage minted when the bytes landed in /raw. Only storage resolves it; data-processing pulls the bytes by ref for ASR.

blob_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex of the blob bytes; end-to-end integrity check against /raw.

device_location.description: Optional (omit when absent). Recording fills where the device has it, else omitted and data-processing infers place from content later. Feeds C2 geo-enrichment (deferred past v0).

device_clock.description: Optional: whether the device wall-clock is NTP-disciplined. Input to data-processing's cross-device skew handling (M4).
